reviews.title: fitbit 3 perforated bands
reviews.text: I purchased these after market bands because the 'large' I got with my fitbit 3 was WAY too big.  It's amazing how much better 1" less in the band fits.  It comes with three colors.  The only thing I am not really super happy about is the silver tone buckle - I am nervous about nickle content as it is an allergy thing for me. The original is black and matches the band. You get 3 full bands, One black... one gray.. and one light aqua blue - it's more of a very light gray blue.  They are a rubbery ? silicone like? material. Smooth, no sharp edges.  They attached easily. So far, so good.  Having said that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

reviews.title: It's ok. But you can get a Fitbit style watch for less
reviews.text: I wear it near my pulse because I feel it gives you a more accurate reading. The sleep monitor sucks. It didn't start monitoring my sleep until I turned it around and put the sensors on my inner wrist

reviews.title: Sent back twice because the blood pressure measurement was inaccurate
reviews.text: I had some confidence in buying this at first.  It connected to app nicely and did well with sleep measurement when I tried that.  I also liked the face choices.  However, having a reasonably accurate blood pressure measurement as long as it is included as a feature was very important for me.  It always read about the same measurement of 140/86, but that was not accurate when compared to a cuff style.  It also read the same for my wife who is always different.  I returned one, got an easy replacement and had the same issue.  Returned for refund this time.  I did not check if waterproof, but that was also an important feature.

reviews.title: doesn't stay on securely
reviews.text: I've purchased one of these in the past and liked it, but the gold color faded so I l reordered another it in black. However, this time, one side kept disconnecting and the attachment site looked defective compared to the other side causing me to almost lose my Fitbit twice. Luckily, I was indoors and heard it drop. I will return it and try a different brand as I fear I will encounter this issue again if I simply replace it.  This style is nice because it's easy to put on and off even with a single hand as opposed to the type of bracelet that comes with the Fitbit.

reviews.title: Worried the magnet will damage device
reviews.text: I wanted a band that would have more variability in fit, and I liked the color, so I ordered this.  It is, indeed, compatible with the Fitbit Sense and I enjoyed it for several days until I had to charge my Sense.  I then discovered that the magnet was so strong that I had difficulty keeping it off the back of my device long enough to get the charger on.  I was worried that strong magnet might damage the circuits and contacted FitBit for their opinion.  They weren't sure, but pointed out that they do have a hook-and-loop wristband in-house that is available.  Since I was also having trouble with the edges of the VNCAS band getting caught on my wool shawl and anything else fuzzy I wore, I decided to ditch it for the FitBit brand option.  Sorry, but I can't recommend this one.

reviews.title: Not Well Made😳
reviews.text: Initially when I bought this watch band, I thought it was nice, solid and seemed to be well made. I've been wearing it just a little over 2 months now and there's some sharp little burrs that have now showed up on the watch band itself and the edges of the other parts are now seeming to be sharp as well and keep getting hung up on my clothing and have also scratched my boyfriend several times. The finish is wearing off on the backside of the magnet connector area so now it looks very tarnished. I do wear it everyday all day long, but I don't feel after such a short period of time it should look so bad and have these sharp edges now. Unfortunately it's past my return window so I'm going to have to just throw it away and get another one. Such a shame because I really like the magnetic band and the color. I'm a little bit leery about buying another similar style for fear that the same thing will happen again. 😳

reviews.title: Cute but it snags
reviews.text: It snags on everything. Ugh. So I can't wear it with nice blouses or sweaters it gets caught and causes holes, runs, etc. Otherwise I love it.

title: SAMSUNG GEAR S3 FRONTIER Smartwatch 46MM (Bluetooth Only) - Dark Grey (Renewed)
description:
Stay in touch, without reaching for your phone, with the smartwatch that’s engineered for adventure. Convenience being the key feature, the watch offers fitness tracking, app access and watch face customization. With the Gear S3, you can pay right from your wrist and track your location as well. The Gear S3 is compatible with both Android and iOS smartphones.
Never Miss a Moment
The Gear S3 is Bluetooth connected so you can receive texts, emails, and alerts without reaching for your phone. With a built-in speaker and microphone, you can also make and answer calls hands-free. Just rotate the distinctive steel bezel to easily access apps and notifications so you can instantly check texts, updates, and more.
Pay with Your Watch
With Samsung Pay on your Gear S3, you can easily make payments with your watch almost anywhere you can swipe or tap a credit card - even without your phone or wallet.
Get Out There
Whether you’re off-roading or exploring a new city, the built-in GPS tracks your activity and makes it easy to share your location.
Designed for The Elements
The military-grade Gear S3 is tough enough to handle the elements. It resists water, dust, extreme temperatures, and the occasional drop so you can venture out with confidence.
Go for Days
Go nonstop for days on a single charge.4 When it’s time to recharge, the wireless charger lets you power up without slowing down.
Tailored to You
Make the Gear S3 your own. Customize your watch face, choosing from thousands of striking colors and designs. Plus easily swap out the standard 22mm band.
features: The Samsung Gear S3 has an always-on watch face and distinctive steel bezel that you can rotate to access apps and notifications.
The Samsung Gear S3 has an always-on watch face and distinctive steel bezel that you can rotate to access apps and notifications.
Text, call and get notifications directly from your watch through your Bluetooth connected smartwatch.
Access the apps you use every day on your Gear S3. Catch a ride, check scores, stream music, and more.
With the built-in S Health app, track your steps, monitor your heart rate, and more.

reviews.title: Just over 90 days and it completely died
reviews.text: Rrally liked this watch at first. The battery did not last very long, even after adjusting settings. Thought it was the battery so we bought a new one, nope just completely died now. The new battery is fine, charges up and works, but the watch will not even turn on with new battery in it.

reviews.title: Love this
reviews.text: Love this watch

reviews.title: Husband LOVES This
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband for his birthday and Father's Day and he loves it. He used to have a Galaxy S9, but dropped it into a lake while we were fishing *face palm*. But he ended up getting an old S7 from a family member and the watch connects with that as well. The only complaint I have heard from him is about the band, which was easy and cheap to replace with a metal material that didn't stick to his wrist as much as the rubbery band that comes with the watch.

reviews.title: Great looking watch!
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband and he loves it!  He answers his phone and text messages right on the watch.  He doesn't have an excuse not to answer me now :D

reviews.title: Good watch
reviews.text:
I bought this when it first came out, I needed it's walking and stair climbing metrics.

It's big, heavy, and if you're at all active, it's on the charge cradle for around 1 hour. The big, heavy part, some like it, I wish it were a little smaller. The battery life, it's an early version, I didn't expect much, my next watch, 20 day minimum.

All in all, it's a very functional watch, feature laden, and good, as long as Samsung supports the OS

reviews.title: So comfortable!
reviews.text: Best band I’ve ever worn! These are so comfortable and lightweight that I sometimes forgot I have my Fitbit on! It’s breathable! reviews.title: Great bands

reviews.title: Easier to set-up than previous bands
reviews.text:
I have really struggled getting replacement bands in place in the past. I had this one switched over in a matter of minutes. The lacy look makes it less apparent that the band is vinyl and the dark colors help to hide stains. I have bad metal allergies nd was a bit afraid of what would happen with the clasp given it is metal and has some contact with my skin. Been wearing this since December and the only time I have issues with a rash was when I had the watch fastened too tightly to my wrist. I believe this was from trapped sweat and moisture and not a metal allergy reaction.
Extra great deal in that you get three bands. I don't foresee a need to buy more bands for at least a year.

reviews.title: Great activity tracker with AMAZING Battery Life.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:e0fda475d57018948d4bdf5116c091bf]] I had a different version activity watch that was way more expensive to match my phone. For the cost this watch does just about everything I need it to for way less money.

A few things I love about the watch...the Battery Life. OMG the battery life! It lasted me a full week with wearing it to bed most nights, through multiple exercise walks and a few work outs, and hundreds of notifications from my messaging apps and phone calls. It's easy to clear the notifications I receive on the watch. I love that it tracks my sleep, heart rate, and stress levels easily. I also tested out in my pool for swimming and it was waterproof without having to shake the watch or turn on any special modes to keep it watertight.

I haven't figured out of it auto detects a work out but that could be because I obviously don't work out that much.

I tested it against my health app on my phone and they were pretty accurate against each other info steps and mileage. I can't figure out how to get the watch and the app to register my GPS coordinates to see the map of my walks but I could be missing the settings.

The app that gives the data is easy to navigate and update any settings. Seems very similar to other track laps I've used.
A few things I'm not liking about this watch...
I feel like the band and the watch itself that comes with the watch is a little too big for my wrist. And I have a bigger wrist for a female at my size. The watch face is bigger than some of my friends apple watches and taller than my old Samsung gear fit watch. I can SEE notifications on the watch but I can't respond or give a quick response to any messages. For Whatsapp or Facebook Messenger I wouldn't expect that but for SMS messages I would like to be able to give a quick text back either by speaking a response or by selecting from some quick options.

Those are really my only 2 complaints. I like this watch and will continue to use!

reviews.title: Such a great Apple Watch dupe!
reviews.text: This smart watch works great! It’s also so cute and the band has lots of holes for adjusting. The only difference I see that’s noticeable is the watch band is a flimsier rubber. But it also makes it feel more comfortable than my Apple Watch. Would make a great gift as well!

reviews.title: Did the job for a week, then it drowned
reviews.text: I charged the watch out of the box.  Followed instructions and setup was pretty much straightforward.  There is a decent amount of sports mode available.  I mainly use run, walk, and swimming.  For walk and run the data captured was okay for duration, calories, distance, number of steps, pace, heart rate. and nighttime monitoring sleep was interesting.  Then I took it for laps in the pool, and that is when it did not do well.  The ad shows person swimming in pool, but I don't believe it can survive in the pool.  because the watch died.

reviews.title: A sluggish, inaccurate, subpar sports/health smart watch.
reviews.text:
I needed a backup sports smart watch for my Apple Watch. Let's check out this Veryfit watch.

What works:
Charged up in about 2-3 hours from 60% battery level.
Easy to install the Veryfit app and pair with my old iPhone 12 Pro.
Has many of the basic sports watch functions: stopwatch, alarm, timer, along with biometrics: Heart rate, Blood oxygen monitoring, Stress level, Breath training, Sleep, etc.
Big, easy to read watch face, only a bit narrower than my 44mm Apple Watch.
A variety of watch faces to download.
Nice, soft, comfortable watch straps.

What don't work:
Once linked up to my iPhone, app takes some effort to set up.
Can only receive notifications and phone call alerts, no relay of calls.
Heart rate monitor is erratic with a wide range of random readings. My resting heart rate is 56bpm yet this watch measures a range of 62 to 125bpm at various times.
Requires about 25s to get each biometric reading.
What's up with the 3-2-1 countdown to start each exercise? Start/Stop is what most users prefer.
Perhaps the Android version works better?
Don't expect too much from this $34.99 smart watch (after -30% off coupon on Feb. 19, 2023) but there are other smart watches in this price range that function better.

reviews.title: I like it
reviews.text: It's a little bulky but not enough for me not to still wear it every day

reviews.title: So glad I found this one!
reviews.text: I am so happy with the way this looks. It doesn’t look a traditional smart watch. It is more stylish. Would purchase again

reviews.title: Looks great but a pain to navigate
reviews.text: I ordered this watch because I love the style. I don’t care for the styles of most smart watches I’ve seen.  This watch looks great, very stylish!!  It comes with a tool to allow you to adjust the band length which is very helpful.  It keeps time perfectly.  It counts your steps and monitors sleep quality. The heart rate and BP monitors seem to be accurate as they are right on with my normal numbers. It measures sleep quality.  The hours I sleep is accurate but I don't know how to tell if the deep vs. light sleep is correct.  I assume its based on heartrate which seems to be accurate.  I cannot get the female app to sync, however,  I don’t use this feature. The apps are a pain to navigate through but aside from the female app they work well. The best part of this watch aside from its ascetics is that the charge lasts four to five days.

title: Garmin Instinct 2, dezl Edition, Rugged Trucking Smartwatch, Easy Break Planning, Compatible with the dezl OTR Navigator, Black
description: Instinct® 2 - dēzl™ Edition is the first rugged smartwatch designed specifically for professional truck drivers who want trackable health metrics, short-break workouts and many truck plaza conveniences for over-the-road lifestyles. Enjoy all-day health monitoring features that track your heart rate, sleep, Pulse Ox, respiration and more (Pulse Ox not available in all countries. This device is intended to give an estimation of your activity and metrics; it is not a medical device). Pair this watch with the dēzl™ app on your compatible smartphone for workout tutorials that fit within standard break times. When using the dēzl app, take advantage of truck plaza loyalty programs with Love’s Travel Stops and Pilot Flying J Travel Centers, and Garmin Pay™ contactless payments help you breeze through checkout lines with a supported country and payment network. reviews.title: Would be the perfect watch if the battery was replaceable
reviews.text:
I normally don't like smart watches because I hate the idea of spending hundreds of dollars on something with a non-replaceable battery that will wear out in a few years rendering the watch useless. Even though I do love my new Instinct 2 watch, I'm still not crazy about the wasteful business model of smart watches, and am not sure I will buy another after this one. That said, the Instinct 2 is a near perfect watch in almost every way... it is feature packed, fully customizable (without being over the top), has a long battery life, and is very comfortable as a daily driver watch.

I use mine for the usual day-to-day watch stuff, and hiking primarily. I don't use any of the smart phone or health features (heart rate, pulse ox, etc.) as I don't care about them. I use the compass, altimeter, and gps tracking features frequently, and they all work great.

Overall, this is a fantastic watch for what it is. However, I really, REALLY hate that it has a non-replaceable battery that will inevitably wear out after only a few years. I know that this is the case as I had a Fenix 5 before this one that lasted about 4 years before the battery began dying quickly. It essentially became a $500 door stop after that...

reviews.title: Smart sports watch with extreme battery life.
reviews.text: In my opinion, this is the perfect smart sports watch. Great battery life, but all the sport features you need. Sure its not color or touch screen, but the utility is as good as it gets.

reviews.title: Great GPS Watch!
reviews.text: So far, so good! I’ve only had this watch for two days, but I’m convinced I made the right choice. I like the color and I have already made a watch face on garmin.facebuilder. It’s new to me but I watched many YouTube videos about this watch, so I knew I’d be able to pick it up quickly. I haven’t been out in the sun enough to appraise the solar capabilities, but I’m quite sure it will be less than they advertised. I’m happy with it just the same! I recommend this watch.

reviews.title: Pretty much a Fenix 6S with a better battery
reviews.text:
I figured I'd give my 6S to the wife and try the 7S Sapphire Solar. In general the watches are nearly identical, but the 7S does have a noticably better battery. When we go hiking, we get identical information, the 7S doesn't need L5 GPS but it works fine. I like the addition of the touch display. Lastly, Garmin is finally getting their act together on software, the 6S and 7S both work well with Garmin Golf and Connect, lots of good data and charts.

There are a few more data pages on the watch for me to look at than on the 6S. I suspect the 6S could have them too but Garmin wants you to upgrade.

Bottom line, if you have a 6, wait for the 8, maybe it will have ECG?

title: Cubitt CT2S Series 3 Smart Watch 1.69" Touch Screen, Fitness Tracker, with Heart Rate, Blood Oxygen, Stress and Sleep Monitor, IP68 Waterproof, Step Counter, Pedometer Smartwatch for Women Men
features: PREMIUM DISPLAY: Large 1.69-inch TFT-LCD color HD touch screen featuring customized displays, wake up function to easily see time, date, steps, calories and distance. SMART FUNCTIONS: Receive Call, Text, Whatsapp and Social Media notifications. Incoming calls can be alerted via vibration reminders, with the option to hang up. Display up to 128 characters on your tracker screen. Sedentary alerts can be turned On, to be alerted with vibration reminders after a specific time of no movement. Alarm clock + timer, and calendar alerts are also shown on the screen as notifications. Option to sync data with Strava

ALL DAY FITNESS TRACKING: Track your daily activities with a built-in accelerometer sensor that allows you to track distance traveled, record steps, get calories burned, and review active minutes, including pace. Your workout progress can be simply viewed directly from our app, by choosing between 14 different sports modes including Outdoor / Outdoor Running and Walking, Outdoor cycling, Spinning bike, Cricket, Hiking, Swimming, Yoga,Rowing machine, Elliptical trainer, Fitness, and others

HEART RATE, BLOOD OXYGEN, SLEEP AND STRESS MONITOR: Automatically monitors your daily heart rate, blood oxygen and stress. You can read directly on the watch all day, which helps you understand your health and make reasonable adjustments on your life style. Also monitors your sleep which gives you a comprenhensive analysis of your sleep. Our app allows you to review graphs that analyze how deep or light is your sleep and your wake-up times.

APP PAIRING AND BATTERY: pairing is simple, by using Bluetooth 4.2, automatically sync your device to a phone using iOS 8. 0/Android OS 4. 4 or above. Built with a 300mAh battery that takes less than 2 hours to charge and can be used for up to 10 days with a standby time of 30-45 days. You can adjust the screen brightness according to your needs

reviews.title: Great watch for a great price
reviews.text: I was skeptical about the watch as I had purchased other watches only to have them fail miserably. This watch turned out great. The most important thing to do is read the manual. Especially about how to wear the watch, which is a little higher than a normal watch. This will make the accuracy much better. The step counter is controlled by arm movement not actual steps, so if you walk stiff armed , it will be inaccurate. Move your arms when walking , the watch will be much more accurate.Make sure you go into the app on your phone and activate the exercises your interested in. This will help the watch to track it better. Also activate the media part of the app for sms,email etc. You can also monitor who is calling you, so you can choose to answer you phone or not. I am extremely pleased with my purchase and will be watching for other products from this company.

reviews.title: As advertised
reviews.text: Great watch! Exactly what I wanted (a watch to count steps and that is easy to read). The set up was fairly easy - I received the watch in the early evening and had it set up within half an hour ( I am in my seventies). This is my 3rd watch - first was very expensive and too invasive - second watch cost twice as much as this one and quit charging in the second week - third times a charm.
